site.title: Frederick Andersen — Keynote Speaker on AI Design Systems & Design Engineering | Figma Config 2026
site.description: Frederick Andersen is a design studio founder and keynote speaker on AI design systems, UX strategy, and design engineering. Speaking at Figma Config 2026. Available for conferences, workshops, and team sessions globally.

intro.text1: Frederick talks design through a technical, commercial, and practical lens

text2.content: He’s a {link}. He works with product teams to build critical, AI-enabled design systems.

talks.title: Design systems without developers
talks.description: How to build production-grade design systems. No dedicated developer required. MCP, LLMs, and a systems mindset developed across 12 months of real client delivery. You leave with a working method, not a slide deck.
talks.audienceFit: Best for: Engineering leads, Design managers, Product teams

tags.tooltip: Hands-on, interactive session format for teams.
Designed for mid-size to large product teams.

actions.primary: Bring me in for a team session

modal.title: Design systems without developers
modal.description: Great for internal design or engineering teams or bigger crowds of technical product folks. Straightforward. No fluff.
modal.subtitle: Frederick's team typically responds within 24 hours.

talks.title: From designers to design engineers
talks.description: EDL cut time-to-market by 48% by making one structural change: designers who ship code became the default. This is what we changed, what it cost us to figure out, and why your team can do it now.
talks.audienceFit: Best for: Design leads, Engineering managers, Product teams

tags.tooltip: Perfect for keynote presentations at conferences and events.
Ideal for founders and product leaders.

modal.description: For teams that want to bridge the gap between design and engineering. Practical, not theoretical.

talks.title: From conversation to commit
talks.description: The best product teams aren't doing more research. They're closing the loop faster. This talk is about building a research-first practice where AI compresses the distance between insight and implementation.

talksPage.title: Talks & Sessions
talksPage.subtitle: Available for conferences, workshops, and team sessions globally.

bookPage.title: The Design Engineering Gap
bookPage.subtitle: A practical guide to closing the space between design and code.
pitch: Most teams treat design and engineering as two separate worlds. One side pushes pixels, the other ships code. The result? Broken handoffs, design drift, and systems that don't scale.

This book is a practical guide for anyone who wants to close that gap. It's built on real production work — not theory — and covers how to think about design systems as engineering artifacts, how to structure cross-functional teams, and how to ship design that actually survives contact with code.
toc: AI-Driven Design: From Prototype to Production
The MCP and LLM Toolkit for Designers

coverImage.placeholder: Book cover — The Design Engineering Gap by Frederick Andersen

aboutPage.title: About Frederick
bio: Frederick Andersen is the founder of EDL, a design studio in Copenhagen that builds AI-driven design systems for technical product teams.
He sits at the intersection of design, engineering, and product strategy — a space where most speakers don't operate. His work is grounded in real production systems, not conference-circuit theory.

Before founding EDL, Frederick worked across product design, front-end engineering, and design systems at scale. He's advised teams at startups and enterprises on how to structure design that actually ships.
Frederick speaks globally on AI design systems, UX strategy, and design engineering. He's the author of The Design Engineering Gap and is speaking at Figma Config 2026.
aboutPage.philosophy: Frederick's talks are built the same way he builds products: with a systems mindset, zero fluff, and a focus on what actually works in production. Every session is grounded in real work — not slides full of theory. He tailors content to the room and leaves teams with tools they can use the next day.
aboutPage.studioDescription: EDL is a design studio that builds AI-driven design systems for technical product teams. Based in Copenhagen, EDL works with startups and scale-ups to create systems that scale.
